free_text: Graphs only
English translation
Numerical data
Correlation between the spectrum of long-range alpha
particles and the fragments in the spontaneous fission
of 252Cf
Cf-252, 8mm-diameter, thickness of carbon backing:
40mu/cm2, source strength: about 1.6E4 fissions/sec.
To identify alpha-particles
between binary fragments and ternary alphas,
correlating four parameters from two fission-fragment
counters and from telescope setup.
To detect the binary fission fragments
25-mu-thick silicon dE detector and a 20-mm
diameter 500-um-thick Si(Au) detector
to register and identify alpha particles.
9.6 mg/cm Al foil between Cf-252 source and
dE detector to prevent registration of fission
fragments and decay alphas in the telescope.
When 4 correlated signals are accepted by the
coincidence unit, the signals are registered in a
4-parameter recorder, forming a data acquisition
system.
The collected data are analyzed by computer, including
all corrections. After corrections, the fission
fragment detectors were calibrated with the spectrum
parameters for binary fission, and the kinetic
energies for each pair of fragments were calculated.
Correlations between the spectra of LRA particles and
fragment mass ratios, as well as between the LRA
spectrum and the total kinetic energy of the fragments
were calculated by computer program.
Corrected for
1)Elimination of events due to decay alphas, light
charged particles other than alphas, and few false
events caused by accidental interference and pileup;
2)Energy loss in the carbon backing and Al foil;
3)Recoil effect originating from alpha emission.
By compiler: The pre-neutron-emission nature of the
data is not explicitly stated in the articles, but
indicated by Fig.2 (caption: Pre-neutron-emission mass
distribution) from which the data in subentry 2 are
derived.
VOF.Only subentry 1, no data available
HW. Reaction code updated in sub. 002
ML. Recompiled subentry 1, added
subentries 3-7 and numerical data for
all subentries

to EXFOR rules.
VS. REACTION code updated in Subent 006.
VS. 007 updated according to CP-D/820.
